Assembly: The assembly ilHipSeme1.2 is based on 58x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. some sequences of chromosome W couldn't be placed and are assigned to the W_unloc bin.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Bivalvia; Autobranchia; Pteriomorphia; Pectinida; Pectinoidea; Pectinidae; Talochlamys
Assembly: The assembly of Talochlamys pusio (xbTalPusi1) is based on 76X PacBio data and 153X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 6 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.463 Mb (with the largest being 0.219 Mb). Additionally, 205 regions totaling 97.776 Mb (with the largest being 5.107 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. During manual curation, 29 haplotypic regions and 2 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 69.180 Mb and 0.149 Mb respectively (with the largest being 12.024Mb and 0.122 Mb).Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Bivalvia; Autobranchia; Pteriomorphia; Pterioida; Pterioidea; Pteriidae; Pinctada
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Bivalvia; Autobranchia; Pteriomorphia; Pterioida; Pterioidea; Pteriidae; Pinctada
Assembly: The xbPinMaxi1.1 assembly represents the genome of a wild Pinctada maxima (silver-lipped pearl oyster) individual, sampled at Roko Pearl Farm, Roko Island, Queensland, Australia. DNA was extracted from adult muscle tissue. Sequencing was performed using PacBio Sequel, 10X Genomics (NovaSeq), and Hi-C. The assembly was generated with Flye, corrected with Hapo-G, and purged with Purge_dups. Hi-C data were mapped with the Arima pipeline, scaffolded with YAHS, and manually curated with JBAT. Transcriptome reads were aligned with Hisat2 and used for gene prediction with Braker3. The genome spans 1.27 Gb in 14 chromosomes (N50: 209.20 Mb).

Assembly: Genome assembly of Bolinus brandaris. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly of Bolinus brandaris (xgBolBran1) is based on 100x PacBio data and Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 535 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 66 Mb (with the largest being 1.4 Mb). Additionally, 2,784 region of 356 Mb were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 1 haplotypic region of 356 kb was removed. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: This project has been funded by the Federation Nationale des Chasseurs (FR). The assembly of Vanellus vanellus (bVanVan3.1) is based on 57X of PacBio data and 36X Dovetail Hi-C data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 19 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.75 Mb (with the largest being 0.28 Mb). Additionally, 353 regions totaling 29 Mb were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed (with the largest being 4 Mb). Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 1 haplotypic regions was removed, totaling 0.74 Mb (with the largest being 0.74 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Sexual chromosome Z was identified using comparative genomics and organization of microchromosomes was curated using MicroFinder.
